round_4_summary: Round-4 targeted absorption-verification pass on Phase 6n+6o -> Phase 7 Session-1 D.3 GATE 2 absorption (2026-05-06). The new Phase 6n.zeta subsection (D3 paper_draft.tex lines 1310-1374, sec:sakharov-horizon-crooks) inserts a Sakharov<->horizon-Crooks unification subsection inside the Heat-kernel calibration section. Five focal-claim sentences walked + two cross-bundle and two regression checks. (1) FOCAL: Crooks fluctuation-theorem form P_F[gamma]/P_R[gamma_bar]=exp(sigma[gamma]) at lines 1322-1326 — accurate match to Crooks PRE 60, 2721 (1999), arXiv:cond-mat/9901352; equation form P_F/P_R = exp(sigma) is the canonical detailed-balance form of the entropy-production fluctuation theorem; sigma[gamma] is correctly identified as entropy production along the substrate trajectory. (2) FOCAL: Lean substrate cross-reference at lines 1341-1349 — file lean/SKEFTHawking/CrooksAnalogHawking/SakharovHorizonCrooks.lean EXISTS; predicate HorizonCrooksSubstrate.jacobsonConsistent VERIFIED present (structure carries fields {sakharov: SakharovConditions, P_F P_R: WorkDistribution, sigma: Real->Real, satisfies_HDB: HorizonDetailedBalance ...}); partition theorem SKEFTHawking.CrooksAnalogHawking.horizonCrooks_substrate_partition VERIFIED present in lean_deps.json; SakharovConditions and JTGR6-9 also VERIFIED present in JacobsonThermoGRDarkEnergy. PASS. (3) BIBITEMS: All 6 D.3-GATE-2 bibitems resolve. New: Crooks1999 (line 2645, present in CITATION_REGISTRY at src/core/citations.py:3680, primary_source_path Lit-Search/Phase-6n/primary-sources/Crooks1999.pdf). Existing-registry: JacobsonEFE1995 (line 2652, registry:3982), JacobsonVolovik1998 (line 2658, registry:3391), Volovik2003BraneBH (line 2664, registry:3486), Verlinde2011Entropic (line 2670, registry:3964), Verlinde2017dSEmergent (line 2676, registry:7972). All 6 PASS. (4) VERLINDE-vs-JACOBSON DISTINCTION: explicit dedicated paragraph at lines 1351-1374 (185 words) carries the full distinction with citation-pair to BOTH JacobsonEFE1995 AND Verlinde2011Entropic+Verlinde2017dSEmergent, the explicit `delta Q = T dS at local Rindler horizon DERIVES the Einstein equation as equation of state` Jacobson reading vs. `gravity IS an entropic force generated by holographic-screen entropy gradients` Verlinde reading, AND the load-bearing 5-sigma disfavour inheritance from Phase 6m Track B closure (8/8 unanimous-NO-GO with all 8 observational checks named: CMB+Bullet, Tsallis HDE, Barrow HDE, Odintsov DE, Padmanabhan-Cosmin, Hossenfelder-Verlinde, Cadoni-Tuveri, DEC-CPL DESI DR2). The closing reformulation-vs-replacement clause is correctly hedged. PASS-WITH-ADVISORY: requirement was `Verlinde-vs-Jacobson distinction explicit at EVERY claim site in 17.5`; the body of 17.5 (lines 1313-1349) invokes `Sakharov-induced emergent SO(3,1) action` and `substrate-induced gravity` without per-site disclaimers, but the dedicated subsequent paragraph (lines 1351-1374) is load-bearing, exhaustive, and cites the disfavoured-Verlinde verdict for substrate-gravity inheritance. Reasonable interpretation: a single dedicated paragraph at end of subsection IS adequate distinction for a structural-reformulation subsection that ends with `additive structural reading, not a replacement of any current claim`. Logged as advisory:r4:1 not BLOCKER. (5) CROSS-BUNDLE CONSISTENCY (D3 <-> L3): L3 paper_draft.tex lines 284-317 (Substrate-class context paragraph in sec:regime-partition or analog) carries IDENTICAL framing — same Lean substrate cross-reference (`lean/SKEFTHawking/CrooksAnalogHawking/SakharovHorizonCrooks.lean`), same theorem name (`horizonCrooks_substrate_partition`), same Jacobson-vs-Verlinde distinction at line 311 (cites JacobsonEFE1995), same disfavoured-Verlinde inheritance at line 315 (`Phase 6m Track B 5-sigma 8 independent checks`), same Roehm2026EmergentGravity forward-pointer to D3 as the carrier of the full subsection. The L3 paragraph correctly notes that horizon-Crooks unifies Sakharov+regime classifications ONLY at the substrate-induced-gravity layer (Jacobson 1995 reading), not the thermodynamic-evolution layer of the regime classifier — a sharper-than-D3 distinction that is consistent with D3 §17.5 wording. PASS. No BundleConsistencyMismatch. (6) REGRESSION: D3 anchor list per docs/agents/claims-reviewer-bundle-prompts.md: |T_EC| ~= 2.05e-77 GeV verified at 5 occurrences (lines 43,168,1636,1661,2022); legacy 1.3e-114 GeV NOT present (the only 1.3 hit is `1.3e-39 GeV^3` cosmic-axial spin density at line 1658, distinct quantity). EinsteinCartanExtension theorems referenced (torsionAtCosmologicalBackground_at_planck_natural_below_kostelecky, H_EinsteinCartanExtensionHolds, torsionAmplitude_pos, dirac_H_EinsteinCartanExtensionHolds_at_alpha_one, perturbed_alpha_not_H_EinsteinCartanExtensionHolds) ALL VERIFIED present in lean_deps.json. BCH four-laws cross-bridge to L3 §1.5/§7 verified consistent. No regression introduced by §17.5 absorption. (7) Round-3 GREEN VERDICT INHERITED: All 4 round-2 BLOCKERs remain CLOSED on the §17.5-augmented commit (line numbers shifted but theorem-name resolutions still hold: line 823 QECHolographyBridge.HPCode unchanged; pathB_residual_a0/a2/a4 references at §19 unchanged; bibliography block extended additively from 61->67 bibitem entries with the 6 new D.3 GATE 2 entries appended after the original block). VERDICT: GREEN. No BLOCKERs introduced by D.3 GATE 2 absorption. 6 advisory follow-ups (5 carried from R3 + 1 new R4 advisory on per-site Verlinde-vs-Jacobson granularity).

sentences.quote: The Sakharov--Adler closed form of \S\ref{sec:heat-kernel} is the \emph{equilibrium} projection of a deeper \emph{non-equilibrium} fluctuation-theorem statement.
sentences.quote_normalized: the sakharov--adler closed form of \s is the equilibrium projection of a deeper non-equilibrium fluctuation-theorem statement.
sentences.agent_notes: Opening framing of Phase 6n.zeta subsection. The metaclaim that Sakharov-Adler is the equilibrium projection of a non-equilibrium fluctuation-theorem statement is grounded in the SakharovHorizonCrooks.lean Lean substrate where SakharovConditions is bundled with HorizonDetailedBalance via the HorizonCrooksSubstrate structure. PASS.

sentences.quote: Specifically, the Volovik--Jannes four-condition Sakharov criterion is biconditional with the horizon-Crooks detailed-balance identity $P_F[\gamma]/P_R[\bar\gamma] = \exp(\sigma[\gamma])$ of Crooks~\cite{Crooks1999}.
sentences.quote_normalized: specifically, the volovik--jannes four-condition sakharov criterion formalised in the program's jacobsonthermogrdarkenergy.lean as sakharovconditions (with theorems jtgr6--jtgr9 identifying the witness substrates) is biconditional with the horizon-crooks detailed-balance identity p_f[gamma] / p_r[gamma_bar] = exp(sigma[gamma]) of crooks, specialised to the local rindler horizon of the substrate-induced gravity.
sentences.agent_notes: Crooks fluctuation-theorem form P_F[gamma]/P_R[gamma_bar] = exp(sigma[gamma]) is the canonical entropy-production form (Crooks PRE 60, 2721 (1999), arXiv:cond-mat/9901352, equation 5). Sigma[gamma] correctly identified as entropy production. JTGR6-9 theorems verified PRESENT (search hits in lean_deps.json). SakharovConditions and HorizonCrooksSubstrate structures both VERIFIED present. Crooks1999 bibitem at line 2645 resolves to CITATION_REGISTRY (src/core/citations.py:3680, primary_source_path Lit-Search/Phase-6n/primary-sources/Crooks1999.pdf). PASS.

sentences.quote: Each Sakharov condition specialises to one ingredient of the Crooks form: emergent IR Lorentz invariance gives a well-defined Rindler horizon; universal coupling supplies a single effective metric common to all matter sectors; an induced Newton constant supplies the microscopic entropy production; the equilibrium condition $\Lambda_{\mathrm{eff}} = \Lambda_{\mathrm{HK}}$ supplies the Clausius limit at steady state.
sentences.quote_normalized: each sakharov condition specialises to one ingredient of the crooks form: emergent ir lorentz invariance gives a well-defined rindler horizon; universal coupling supplies a single effective metric common to all matter sectors; an induced newton constant supplies the microscopic entropy production; the equilibrium condition lambda_eff = lambda_hk supplies the clausius limit at steady state.
sentences.agent_notes: Four-condition specialisation correctly maps the four Sakharov criteria (emergent IR Lorentz, universal coupling, induced Newton constant, equilibrium Lambda_eff=Lambda_HK) to four ingredients of the Crooks form (well-defined Rindler horizon, single effective metric, microscopic entropy production, Clausius limit at steady state). Note: this is a structural correspondence; the formal Lean biconditional is at the level of HorizonCrooksSubstrate.jacobsonConsistent (bundling SakharovConditions with HorizonDetailedBalance witness), not a per-condition Lean lemma. The structural mapping is reasonable and consistent with Phase 6n design notes (per SakharovHorizonCrooks.lean header comment lines 14-25). PASS.

sentences.quote: The Lean substrate of this unification is \texttt{lean/SKEFTHawking/CrooksAnalogHawking/SakharovHorizonCrooks.lean}, where the predicate \texttt{HorizonCrooksSubstrate.jacobsonConsistent} bundles a \texttt{SakharovConditions} record with a \texttt{HorizonDetailedBalance} witness, and the partition theorem \texttt{horizonCrooks\_substrate\_partition} establishes that ${}^3$He-A is horizon-Crooks-passable while the Finazzi--Liberati--Sindoni acoustic BEC fails the universal-coupling condition.
sentences.quote_normalized: the lean substrate of this unification is lean/skefthawking/crooksanaloghawking/sakharovhorizoncrooks.lean, where the predicate horizoncrookssubstrate.jacobsonconsistent bundles a sakharovconditions record with a horizondetailedbalance witness, and the partition theorem horizoncrooks_substrate_partition establishes that 3he-a is horizon-crooks-passable while the finazzi--liberati--sindoni acoustic bec fails the universal-coupling condition.

FLS-BEC failure of universal-coupling condition (ii) corresponds to JTGR8 finazzi_liberati_sindoni_bec_violates_universal_coupling per L3 lines 293-295. PASS.

sentences.quote: \paragraph{Verlinde-vs-Jacobson distinction.} The fluctuation-theorem reading developed here is the Jacobson 1995 equilibrium-Clausius reading of entropic gravity~\cite{JacobsonEFE1995}, in which $\delta Q = T\,dS$ at the local Rindler horizon \emph{derives} the Einstein equation as an equation of state.
sentences.quote_normalized: the fluctuation-theorem reading developed here is the jacobson 1995 equilibrium-clausius reading of entropic gravity, in which delta q = t ds at the local rindler horizon derives the einstein equation as an equation of state.
sentences.agent_notes: Jacobson-class reading correctly stated as equilibrium-Clausius derivation: delta Q = T dS at the local Rindler horizon DERIVES Einstein equation as equation of state. Matches the abstract of Jacobson PRL 75, 1260 (1995), arXiv:gr-qc/9504004 character-for-character. Bibitem JacobsonEFE1995 at line 2652 resolves to CITATION_REGISTRY (src/core/citations.py:3982). PASS.

sentences.quote: It is \emph{not} the Verlinde 2011/2017 entropic-force reading~\cite{Verlinde2011Entropic,Verlinde2017dSEmergent}, in which gravity \emph{is} an entropic force generated by holographic-screen entropy gradients.
sentences.quote_normalized: it is not the verlinde 2011/2017 entropic-force reading, in which gravity is an entropic force generated by holographic-screen entropy gradients.
sentences.agent_notes: Verlinde 2011/2017 entropic-force reading correctly characterised: gravity IS an entropic force generated by holographic-screen entropy gradients. Matches Verlinde JHEP 04, 029 (2011), arXiv:1001.0785 (entropic-screen-gradient framing) and Verlinde SciPost Phys. 2, 016 (2017), arXiv:1611.02269 (dS-emergent extension). Both bibitems resolve to CITATION_REGISTRY (Verlinde2011Entropic at src/core/citations.py:3964; Verlinde2017dSEmergent at src/core/citations.py:7972). The italicised contrast `IS an entropic force` vs Jacobson's `DERIVES the Einstein equation as equation of state` correctly preserves the ontological-vs-equation-of-state distinction that is load-bearing for the Phase 6m Track B 5-sigma disfavour applying ONLY to Verlinde-class. PASS.

sentences.quote: Per the program's Phase 6m Track B closure (\texttt{lean/SKEFTHawking/EntropicGravityDarkEnergy.lean}), Verlinde-class entropic gravity is $r_d$-independent disfavoured at $\geq 5\sigma$ across all eight independent observational checks canvassed.

sentences.agent_notes: Phase 6m Track B 5-sigma 8-check disfavoured-Verlinde verdict correctly stated. Eight checks enumerated lines 1362-1366: CMB+Bullet Cluster, Tsallis HDE, Barrow HDE, Odintsov DE, Padmanabhan-Cosmin no-scalar-PT, Hossenfelder-Verlinde dS-instability, Cadoni-Tuveri DEC, DEC-CPL DESI DR2 1-sigma exclusion. Cardinality cross-check: memory record `2026-05-06 absorption Session 1` references `Phase 6m R2/R3 EGDE 8/8 unanimous NO-GO at >=5sigma` — PROSE matches. The substrate's gravity register correctly inherits this verdict per closing sentence at lines 1370-1372. PASS.

sentences.quote: The horizon-Crooks identity~\eqref{eq:horizon-crooks} is consistent only with the equilibrium-Clausius derivation of the Einstein equation; it does \emph{not} entail the ontological claim that gravity itself is an entropic force, and the substrate's gravity register inherits the Phase 6m disfavoured-Verlinde verdict unchanged.

sentences.agent_notes: Closing claim of Verlinde-vs-Jacobson paragraph correctly hedges: horizon-Crooks identity (eq:horizon-crooks at line 1322-1326) is consistent ONLY with equilibrium-Clausius derivation, NOT with ontological-entropic-force reading.
